Notice Of Motion And Motion For Reconsideration On The Sanctions Order
Off Calendar Apr-07-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT NAIM JAMALI'S Motion For Reconsideration On The Sanctions Order.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Matter is transferred to be heard on the discovery calendar at 9:00 a.m. on the same date.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com (302/RBU)

Defendant Naim Jamalis' Motion For Reconsideration On The Sanctions Order.
Off Calendar Apr-07-2022 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
Cathy Mosbrucker, Esq. (415.398.9880; cmosbrucker@gmail.com) of Mosbrucker & Foran for Plaintiff.
Christine Fenech, Esq. (415.362.2580; Christine.Reed@lewisbrisbois.com) of Lewis Brisbois Bisgaard & Smith for Defendants.
Philip Segal, Esq. (415.474.1900; phil@kernlaw.com) of Kern Segal & Murray for and with Defendant, Mr. Jamali.

Hearing is held. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

Defendant Naim Jamalis' Motion For Reconsideration On The Sanctions Order is DENIED for 1)untimely 2) no new facts presented.

Prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling and in compliance with CRC 3.1312 via email to williamclynn@gmail.com.

Judge Pro Tem: William Lynn, Clerk: S. Le, Not reported. =(302/JPT)

MOTION TO CONTINUE JURY TRIAL
Off Calendar Mar-08-2022 Text Ruling

Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Interogg
Off Calendar Feb-17-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. CATHY MOSBRUCKER, Esq. of Mosbrucker & Foran (Tel: 415-398-9880) for plaintiffs;
Attys. CHRISTINE BALBO REED, Esq. and MADISON JUSCHIN, Esq. of Lewis Brisbois Bisgaard & Smith LLP (Tel: 415-362-2580) for defendants Naim Jamali and Sana M. Jamali.

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFFS MARITES BALLESTEROS, JONATHAN BALLESTEROS, and JOHN SAYBE's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatories.

Parties stipulate to the matter being decided by the pro tem judge. The pro tem judge and counsel for parties (all appearing remotely) authorize the clerk to sign the formal stipulation form on their behalf.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court rules as follows: Plaintiffs' motion is denied as moot based on the representation of counsel that all outstanding discovery has now been produced. Plaintiffs' requests for sanctions is granted in the amount of $3,325.00 as to defendant Naim Jamali only (not as to defense counsel) which is to be paid within 20 days of notice of entry of the court's order.

Counsel for plaintiffs to prepare a proposed order and email it to the pro tem judge at williamclynn@gmail.com.

Judge Pro Tem: William Lynn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/JPT)

Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Interogg

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Matter is continued on the court's own motion to February 17, 2022 at 9:00 a.m. Recusal by Judge Pro Tem Peter Catalanotti.

Parties are reminded if they have not done so already to comply with the instructions posted on the department's website (https://sfsuperiorcourt.org/divisions/civil/law-motion) to attach electronic copies of their discovery motion papers to an email addressed to Discovery302@sftc.org. The subject line of the email should state the case name and number and the continued hearing date.

Judge Pro Tem: Peter Catalanotti; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/JPT)
